String.signature: abstract class String implements Comparable<String>, Pattern
String.description: A sequence of UTF-16 code units representing text in Dart. Strings are immutable and support interpolation with $variable and ${expression} syntax. The String class provides comprehensive methods for searching, splitting, trimming, padding, and transforming text.

length.description: Returns the number of UTF-16 code units in the string, which may differ from the number of visible characters for non-BMP code points.

contains.description: Returns true if this string contains a match of the specified pattern (string or RegExp) starting from the given index.

split.description: Splits the string at each match of the pattern and returns a list of substrings.

substring.description: Returns the substring from start index (inclusive) to end index (exclusive), or to the end if omitted.

trim.description: Returns a new string with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

replaceAll.description: Replaces all matches of the pattern in the string with the replacement string.

toUpperCase.description: Converts all characters in the string to uppercase according to the locale-independent rules.

startsWith.description: Returns true if the string begins with a match of the pattern at the specified index.

int.description: An integer number type in Dart. On native platforms, values can be from -2^63 to 2^63-1. On the web, values are represented as JavaScript numbers with up to 53 bits of precision. Supports standard arithmetic operations, bitwise operations, and conversion methods.

parse.signature: static int parse(String source, {int? radix, int onError(String source)?})
parse.description: Parses a string as an integer literal in the given radix (default 10), throwing FormatException if invalid.

tryParse.description: Parses a string as an integer, returning null instead of throwing if the string is not a valid integer.

toDouble.description: Converts this integer to a double-precision floating-point representation.

toString.description: Returns the decimal string representation of this integer.

toRadixString.description: Converts this integer to a string representation in the given radix (base 2-36).

double.description: A double-precision 64-bit IEEE 754 floating-point number in Dart. Doubles are used for all floating-point arithmetic and include special values like double.infinity, double.negativeInfinity, and double.nan for handling edge cases.

parse.signature: static double parse(String source, [double onError(String source)?])
parse.description: Parses the source string as a double literal, throwing FormatException if it is not a valid representation.

toStringAsFixed.description: Returns the decimal string representation with exactly fractionDigits digits after the decimal point.

round.description: Returns the integer closest to this double, rounding half-values away from zero.

List.description: An indexable collection of objects with a zero-based integer index. Lists are the most commonly used collection type in Dart for ordered data. They can be fixed-length (created with List.filled) or growable (created with [] literal or List.empty(growable: true)).

add.description: Appends a single element to the end of the list, increasing its length by one.

addAll.description: Appends all objects of the iterable to the end of this list.

where.description: Returns a lazy iterable of all elements that satisfy the given predicate test function.

map.description: Returns a lazy iterable with each element transformed by the given function.

sort.signature: void sort([int compare(E a, E b)?])
sort.description: Sorts the list in-place according to the compare function, or natural ordering if omitted.

contains.description: Returns true if the collection contains an element equal to the given element.

removeAt.description: Removes and returns the element at the given index, shifting subsequent elements down.

forEach.description: Applies the given function to each element of the list in iteration order.

Map.description: A collection of key-value pairs from which values are retrieved using their associated keys. Maps in Dart support generic key and value types, iteration over entries, and various constructors including Map.from, Map.of, and Map.fromIterables.

containsKey.description: Returns true if this map contains the given key.

putIfAbsent.description: Looks up the value for the key, or adds a new entry if the key is absent by calling ifAbsent.

remove.description: Removes the key and its associated value from the map and returns the value.

forEach.signature: void forEach(void action(K key, V value))
forEach.description: Applies the given function to each key-value pair in the map.

entries.description: Returns an iterable of all key-value pairs as MapEntry objects.

keys.description: Returns an iterable of all keys in the map.

Set.description: A collection of unique objects where each object can occur only once. Sets provide efficient membership testing (contains) and support union, intersection, and difference operations for combining sets.

add.description: Adds the value to the set. Returns true if the value was not already in the set.

contains.description: Returns true if the set contains the specified value.

union.description: Returns a new set containing all elements from both sets.

intersection.description: Returns a new set with only the elements present in both sets.

difference.description: Returns a new set with elements in this set that are not in the other set.

Future.description: An object representing a delayed computation whose result will be available at some later time. Futures are the foundation of Dart's asynchronous programming model, used with async/await syntax for non-blocking I/O, network requests, file operations, and any operation that takes time to complete.

then.description: Registers a callback to be called when this future completes with a value, returning a new future for chaining.

catchError.description: Handles errors emitted by this future, optionally filtering by error type with the test function.

whenComplete.description: Registers a function to be called when the future completes, whether with a value or an error (like finally).

timeout.description: Returns a future that completes with a timeout error if this future does not complete within the time limit.

wait.signature: static Future<List<T>> wait<T>(Iterable<Future<T>> futures, {bool eagerError = false})
wait.description: Waits for multiple futures to complete and returns their results as a list in the same order.

Stream.description: A source of asynchronous data events that delivers a sequence of values over time. Streams are used for handling continuous data sources like user input events, WebSocket messages, file reads, and timer ticks. Supports single-subscription and broadcast stream types.

listen.signature: StreamSubscription<T> listen(void onData(T event)?, {Function? onError, void onDone()?, bool? cancelOnError})
listen.description: Subscribes to events from this stream, providing callbacks for data, error, and completion events.

map.description: Creates a new stream that transforms each data event from this stream using the given function.

where.description: Creates a new stream that only includes data events that satisfy the predicate.

first.description: Returns a future that completes with the first element of the stream.

toList.description: Collects all data events from the stream into a list, returned when the stream closes.

Iterable.description: A collection of values that can be accessed sequentially. Iterable is the base interface for all collections in Dart and defines lazy operations like map, where, expand, take, and skip that are only evaluated when the result is iterated.

map.description: Returns a lazy iterable where each element is the result of applying the given function.

where.description: Returns a lazy iterable of elements that satisfy the predicate test function.

fold.signature: T fold<T>(T initialValue, T combine(T previousValue, E element))
fold.description: Reduces the collection to a single value by iteratively combining the elements with an accumulator.

toList.description: Creates a List containing the elements of this iterable in iteration order.

DateTime.description: An instant in time representing a date and time-of-day with microsecond precision. DateTime objects can represent UTC or local time and provide methods for date arithmetic, comparison, formatting, and extracting components like year, month, day, hour, minute, and second.

now.description: Creates a DateTime object representing the current date and time in the local time zone.

parse.description: Parses a string conforming to a subset of ISO 8601 format into a DateTime object.

difference.description: Returns the difference between this and another DateTime as a Duration object.

add.description: Returns a new DateTime with the given duration added to this one.

isBefore.description: Returns true if this DateTime occurs before the other DateTime.

toIso8601String.description: Returns an ISO-8601 full-precision extended format representation of this DateTime.
